excerpt: the Imāms (upon whom be peace) who have transmitted the Sharīʿa (divine law) and guidance to their followers
source_location_hint: on the spiritual guides and their role in the daʿwa

excerpt: in it sustenance is distributed among the creatures, the decree of death for those destined to meet Allah is inscribed, and there occurs a night in which righteous action surpasses the deeds of a thousand months
source_location_hint: on the virtues of Ramadan and the Night of Power

excerpt: Verily, to Allah belongs origination and will in all things, save only in the Imāmate
source_location_hint: on divine decree and the exception of Imamate

excerpt: Whoever provides sustenance to a fasting person so that he may break his fast, Allah shall forgive his sins and liberate him from the Fire of Hell
source_location_hint: on the reward of feeding the fasting

excerpt: when the Imām removes those among his followers whose spiritual perfection has expired, he extracts them and entrusts them to those of lower rank for further cultivation
source_location_hint: on the Imām's governance of the spiritual hierarchy

excerpt: Through these sacred supplications, proofs, and Qur'ānic chapters, our Imām (upon whom be peace) reaches us just as the transition from night to day is established
source_location_hint: on the spiritual rank of night and the Imām's guidance

excerpt: In the testimony of faith the letters alif and lām appear five times; the takbīra is the mysterious wisdom of Allah repeated five times
source_location_hint: on the numerical symbolism of the glorifications
